articles.title: UAP forum will let public hear from lawmakers, whistleblowers on disclosure 2026 | JUSTICE - IUSTITIA.BG 2025
articles.summary: The Disclosure Forum 2026, held Thursday in Washington, D.C., brought together nearly two dozen lawmakers, former intelligence officials, researchers, and whistleblowers to discuss UAP disclosure. Panels and roundtables, themed “Humanity at the Edge of Discovery,” covered security, defense, and policy implications and featured speakers such as Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand, Rep. Eric Burlison, and Rep. Tim Burchett. Disclosure Foundation board members Professor Avi Loeb and Mike Gold spoke, with Gold noting that a poll shows 89 % of Republicans and 88 % of Democrats want more UAP information and describing UAP as a national security issue. Loeb, recently appointed by the White House to lead the UAP Science Advisory Council, said the federal government must allocate resources to analyze existing data and gather clearer new data on UAP. The forum also highlighted a 2015 Department of Defense video labeled Gimbal that shows an unexplained object, underscoring the need for further investigation. Some advisory council members are skeptical of UAP claims, indicating that belief in UAP is not universal among participants.

de.summary: Das Disclosure Forum 2026, das am Donnerstag in Washington, D.C. stattfand, brachte fast zwei Dutzend Gesetzgeber, ehemalige Geheimdienstmitarbeiter, Forscher und Whistleblower zusammen, um über die Offenlegung von UAP zu diskutieren. In den Podiumsdiskussionen und Rundtischgesprächen unter dem Motto „Die Menschheit am Rande der Entdeckung“ ging es um Fragen zu Sicherheit, Verteidigung und Politik. Redner waren unter anderem Senatorin Kirsten Gillibrand, Rep.

articles.title: Cosmic Council: Trump creates UFO panel to be advised by Harvard's Avi Loeb
articles.summary: The Trump administration has created a new UAP Governance Board to address unidentified anomalous phenomena, holding its first meeting on Tuesday. The board’s mission is to support the President’s directive on UAP transparency, integrate interagency processes, and coordinate the declassification of UAP information among military, law enforcement, intelligence, and civilian agencies. Harvard astrophysicist Avi Loeb has been appointed as an advisor and will lead the board’s UAP Science Advisory Council, a smaller working group focused on data-driven analysis. Loeb, who previously highlighted the interstellar object 3I/Atlas as a possible alien probe, says the program will have no budget, rely only on declassified data, and avoid public distraction. The board may investigate cases such as the 2023 glowing mothership orb near Cheyenne Mountain, and Loeb notes that 40% of observed phenomena cannot be explained by known U.S. or adversary technology. Loeb’s advisory team includes scientists Michael Shermer, Gary Nolan, Kevin Knuth, Matthew Szydagis, and retired Rear Admiral Gallaudet, who says the government is overdue to adopt a scientific perspective on UAP.

en.title: Cosmic Council: Trump creates UFO panel to be advised by Harvard's Avi Loeb
en.summary: The Trump administration has created a new UAP Governance Board to address unidentified anomalous phenomena, holding its first meeting on Tuesday. The board’s mission is to support the President’s directive on UAP transparency, integrate interagency processes, and coordinate the declassification of UAP information among military, law enforcement, intelligence, and civilian agencies.

de.title: Kosmischer Rat: Trump gründet ein UFO-Gremium, das von Harvard-Mitarbeiter Avi Loeb beraten wird
de.summary: Die Trump-Administration hat ein neues UAP-Governance-Board eingerichtet, das sich mit nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen befassen soll, und hält am Dienstag seine erste Sitzung ab. Die Aufgabe des Gremiums besteht darin, die Richtlinie des Präsidenten zur UAP-Transparenz zu unterstützen, behördenübergreifende Prozesse zu integrieren und die Freigabe von UAP-Informationen zwischen Militär, Strafverfolgungsbehörden, Geheimdiensten und zivilen Behörden zu koordinieren.

articles.title: Disclosure Day (2026) Box Office performance crashes like a UFO in Roswell
articles.summary: Steven Spielberg's alien invasion film "Disclosure Day" opened in theaters on June 12, 2026. After just under two weeks, the movie has earned $162.7 million worldwide, with only $80.8 million coming from North America. The film's revenue fell 62 % in its second weekend, and its $115 million production cost means it would need about $280 million at the box office to break even. Because of the weak performance, Universal may pull the film from theatrical rotation if the trend continues, while the studio plans to accelerate online and digital releases. The movie stars Emily Blunt and is still currently playing in theaters, encouraging viewers to book tickets before it potentially closes.

en.summary: Steven Spielberg's alien invasion film "Disclosure Day" opened in theaters on June 12, 2026. After just under two weeks, the movie has earned $162.7 million worldwide, with only $80.8 million coming from North America. The film's revenue fell 62 % in its second weekend, and its $115 million production cost means it would need about $280 million at the box office to break even.

de.title: Disclosure Day (2026) Die Vorstellung an den Kinokassen stürzt wie ein UFO in Roswell ab
de.summary: Steven Spielbergs Alien-Invasionsfilm „Disclosure Day“ startete am 12. Juni 2026 in den Kinos. Nach knapp zwei Wochen hat der Film weltweit 162,7 Millionen US-Dollar eingespielt, davon kamen nur 80,8 Millionen US-Dollar aus Nordamerika. Die Einnahmen des Films gingen am zweiten Wochenende um 62 % zurück, und die Produktionskosten von 115 Millionen US-Dollar bedeuten, dass an den Kinokassen etwa 280 Millionen US-Dollar nötig wären, um die Gewinnschwelle zu erreichen.

articles.title: “They plan to eliminate you:” Lue Elizondo reports Congress threats for UAP whistleblowing
articles.summary: Lue Elizondo, a former Pentagon official, says he received phone calls from Congress workers warning that he and fellow UAP whistleblower David Grusch were targeted with a “plan to eliminate” them. The calls, one from an inebriated staffer who had left a confidential UAP briefing and another warning about bureaucratic disapproval, prompted Elizondo to heavily arm his home and keep five German shepherds for protection. On Sean Hannity’s podcast, Elizondo explained that UAP whistleblowers receive little government protection and that the government keeps UAP information secret to shield foreign adversaries, avoid public panic, and preserve social order, citing a RAND study and a White House committee debate. He warned that public disclosure of UAPs could trigger lawsuits from defense contractors claiming unfair denial of advanced technology, potentially creating a “legal cottage industry.” Elizondo also alleged that some elected leaders, including the president, were kept out of the loop on the issue and called for greater congressional oversight and whistleblower protections.

articles.title: World UFO Day invites believers, skeptics and the curious to look to the skies
articles.summary: World UFO Day is observed each year on July 2 and commemorates the 1947 Roswell incident in New Mexico. The day encourages discussion of unidentified flying objects, now referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs), and reflects growing public interest fueled by military videos and congressional attention. While many sightings are later identified as aircraft, weather phenomena, or satellites, some remain officially unidentified, and enthusiasts celebrate with festivals, film viewings, sky scanning, and story sharing. Scientists note that an unidentified object does not equal evidence of extraterrestrial life, as it simply means insufficient information to determine what was observed.
